question.text: An insider made profits of Rs. 12 crore through insider trading. What is the maximum penalty under Section 15G?

answer.text: Rs. 36 crore (three times the profits of Rs. 12 crore, which exceeds Rs. 25 crore)
Rs. 12 crore (equal to the profits made)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15G states the maximum penalty is twenty-five crore rupees OR three times the profits made from insider trading, WHICHEVER IS HIGHER. Three times Rs. 12 crore = Rs. 36 crore, which exceeds Rs. 25 crore. So the maximum is Rs. 36 crore. **The Trap:** The Rs. 25 crore is not an absolute cap — when three times the profits exceeds Rs. 25 crore, the higher amount applies. This prevents large-scale insider trading from being profitable. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The "three times profits" provision is a powerful deterrent — it ensures insider trading can never be economically rational. Compliance officers should highlight this in their ethics training programs.
